description: island country in East Asia

description: school of Mahāyāna Buddhist Tradition
description_wiki: Zen (Japanese pronunciation: [dzeꜜɴ, dzeɴ]; from Chinese: Chan; in Korean: Sŏn, and Vietnamese: Thiền) is a Mahayana Buddhist tradition that developed in China during the Tang dynasty by blending Indian Mahayana Buddhism, particularly Yogacara and Madhyamaka philosophies, with Chinese Taoist thought, especially Neo-Daoist.

description: defunct American Class I railway company (1889–1970)
description_wiki: The Great Northern Railway (reporting mark GN) was an American Class I railroad.

description: defunct transcontinental railroad company in the northwest United States (1864-1970)
description_wiki: The Northern Pacific Railway (reporting mark NP) was an important American transcontinental railroad that operated across the northern tier of the Western United States, from Minnesota to the Pacific Northwest between 1864 and 1970.

description: city in Lincoln County, Montana, USA

description_wiki: Spokane (  spoh-KAN; Spokane: sƛ̓x̌etkʷ) is a city in and the county seat of Spokane County in the U.S. state of Washington.

description: West Germanic language
description_wiki: English is a West Germanic language in the Indo-European language family.

description: Japanese family name (池田)
description_wiki: Ikeda (written: 池田) is the 26th most common Japanese surname.

description: Indian religion
description_wiki: Buddhism, also known as Buddha Dharma and Dharmavinaya, is an Indian religion and philosophy based on teachings attributed to the Buddha, a śramaṇa and religious teacher who lived in the 6th or 5th century BCE. It is the world's fourth-largest religion, with about 320 million followers, known as Buddhists, who comprise 4.1% of the global population.

description_wiki: The Old Testament (OT) is the first division of the Christian biblical canon, which is based primarily upon the 24 books of the Hebrew Bible, or Tanakh, a collection of ancient religious Hebrew and occasionally Aramaic writings by the Israelites.

aliases: Our father who art in Heaven
description: central Christian prayer, taught by Jesus Christ to his disciples
description_wiki: The Lord's Prayer, also known by its incipit Our Father (Greek: Πάτερ ἡμῶν, romanized: Páter hēmôn; Latin: Pater Noster), is a central Christian prayer attributed to Jesus.

description: family name
